what_it_does: This file documents a provenance and freshness metadata block for a ledger noticeboard item, listing fields such as author_session, created/updated timestamps, and target_version, and noting that the board supplies timestamps while the content-version axis is already handled in address.py.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, board address, type block, source claude_code, state current, title P13, channel dragnet-development, and a part_of link to item-e42d651f.
Body is a markdown heading followed by a template snippet with placeholder values in angle brackets.
It references address.py as the place where the general content-version axis already lives.
It uses the term split_version in connection with target_version @v<n>.
No executable code or symbols are present.
standouts: It is a design-substrate noticeboard item rather than source code.
It explicitly partitions metadata responsibilities between the board and address.py.
It uses placeholders for session id, ISO timestamps, and version number.
conventions: Ledger-style YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
board:// URI references for linked items.
session:// URI for the author session.
Markdown body with a level-1 heading and a code-like metadata block.
concerns: The content uses placeholder values, so the exact schema and validation rules are not defined here.
It does not specify how freshness or provenance will be enforced or verified.
The relationship between split_version and address.py is mentioned but not explained.
notes: A concise design note that enumerates provenance and freshness fields for ledger items and clarifies which ones the board manages versus which ones live in address.py.
questions: What is the exact format and validation rule for target_version?
How does split_version relate to the version axis in address.py?
Is author_session mandatory for every noticeboard item?
What is the parent item-e42d651f and how does this item fit into it?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document provenance and freshness metadata, and the file does exactly that by listing the relevant fields and their management responsibilities.
apparent_themes: Separation of concerns between board-managed and code-managed metadata
intention_signals: It appears to be establishing a minimal, reusable provenance/freshness header pattern for design substrates, making it clear that temporal metadata is board-provided while content versioning belongs in address.py.
novelty: It elevates provenance and freshness to an explicitly enumerated metadata layer for design substrates, rather than relying solely on implicit version-control history.
contribution: Provides a concrete field list and responsibility split for provenance/freshness metadata that a fused ledger system could adopt or reconcile.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design item that enumerates provenance and freshness metadata fields for ledger items and notes that timestamps are board-provided while content versioning is handled in address.py.
intention_for_embedding: To standardize how authorship, creation and update time, and target version are recorded and partitioned across the noticeboard and address system.
